channel.title: Critical-Photon on Dyson Sphere Program Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Critical-Photon on Dyson Sphere Program Guide

item.title: DSP Dyson Sphere Layer Design (v0.10) - Permanent Frame Strategy for Maximum Power Per Solar Sail
item.description:
Short answer: Build the Dyson Sphere frame first, then fill with sails. One complete frame layer with ~200 nodes produces enough Critical Photons for 100+ MW of antimatter power. Use the closest orbit to the star (0.5 AU or less) for maximum energy output per sail. Each inner orbit layer produces ~40% more power than the next ring outward.

The Dyson Sphere is the ultimate goal of DSP, but most players build it inefficiently. They launch sails into random orbits where they expire after 2 hours, wasting resources. The correct approach is frame-first: build the permanent structural frame, then let sails fill it.

item.title: 1 Artificial Sun Replaces 125 Wind Turbines - DSP Endgame Power Loop Guide
item.description:
Critical photons and antimatter are the gateway to endgame power in DSP. The loop is confusing at first: photons come from the sphere, make antimatter, make fuel rods, power your planet. Here is exactly how the chain works, step by step.

TL;DR: The Endgame Power Loop

Ray Receivers capture Dyson Sphere power and output Critical Photons. Particle Colliders turn photons into Antimatter + Hydrogen. Assemblers combine Antimatter with other materials to make Deuterium Fuel Rods or Antimatter Fuel Rods. Artificial Suns burn these rods for massive power. The loop produces more power than it consumes - but only if designed correctly.
